test_qa05_hallucination_no_impossible_success_on_wrong_otp
Test function
test_qa05_hallucination_no_impossible_success_on_wrong_otp
What it checks
Wrong OTP must NOT produce a login success message (hallucinated success). Note: 'Welcome back' is the modal *title* — it is shown before any login attempt, so we exclude it from the hallucinated-success phrase list. The test only flags phrases that imply a *completed* login.
Test data
fill("000000") · TEST_COUNTRY_CODE · TEST_PHONE · TEST_USER_NAME
Duration
11.96s
▶
✓
test_qa05_error_garbage_not_shown_on_modal_failure
Test function
test_qa05_error_garbage_not_shown_on_modal_failure
What it checks
After any modal error, raw JSON / stack traces must not be visible. Resilient to OTP rate-limit (HTTP 429): we always check the visible page for garbage content, even if Send-Code/Continue could not be clicked.
Test data
fill("000000") · TEST_COUNTRY_CODE · TEST_PHONE
Duration
24.19s

QA-05 -001 HIGH P1
Failed: Test qa05 consistent page after language round trip
https://dev.mehadedu.com/en test_qa05_consistent_page_after_language_round_trip 2026-06-12 03:06:56 6.77s
What this test was checking
EN → AR → EN round-trip must return to clean homepage without phantom state — verified against https://dev.mehadedu.com/en.
Test data used
BASE_URL
Steps to Reproduce
  1. Open a browser and go to https://dev.mehadedu.com/en
  2. Observe the result: it does NOT match what the test expected (see Actual Behavior)
What should happen
EN → AR → EN round-trip must return to clean homepage without phantom state.
What actually happened
The test waited for an element to appear, but it never showed up within 5000ms. The element may not exist on the page, or the page is loading very slowly.
